goal: Document the evidence that Step 3’s Research Pipeline has executable foundations by extracting the acceptance criteria, required topology, and runtime scope from the local artifacts.
scope: Extract the acceptance criteria, execution topology, and runtime constraints for the Research Pipeline from the three specified local files.
summary: Step 3 requires running a Research Pipeline composed of Planner/Retriever/Verifier/Synthesizer agents that produce the prescribed structured JSON with fallback guidance, interface contracts, and state_delta consolidation, all within the constrained local-only execution scope defined by the research_pipeline_input.

facts: Research Pipeline must run Planner/Retriever/Verifier/Synthesizer agents and output schema-enforced JSON with goal/scope/evidence/state_delta/next_steps/fallback fields.
Structured JSON must survive schema validation and include fallback_suggestion for failure cases while respecting memory/write boundaries.
Runtime scope confines the execution to local documents and artifacts listed in the research_pipeline_input, preventing external network access.
preferences: Favor local document artifacts as the sole evidence sources when proving Step 3 readiness.
Emphasize schema-backed outputs and interface contracts to demonstrate executable grounding.
decisions: Use the provided README and enhancement guide to establish acceptance criteria and topology requirements without modifying files.
Rely on the research_pipeline_input to define runtime scope and expected checks for validation.
risks: No runtime execution data is present, so the conclusion is based purely on documentation references.
Evidence is entirely documentary; there is no real execution trace to confirm the Research Pipeline has already run.
next_steps: Share these curated acceptance criteria with the main agent so it can orchestrate an actual Research Pipeline execution against the documented topology.
If additional runtime confirmation is needed, request the main agent to run validate_and_merge.py with the Research Pipeline JSON outputs and report results.
